title: Dependency Types V1
description: A versioned envelope describing a typed dependency edge between entities across ecosystem tools. Tracks edges such as blocking, relating, discovery, superseding, and duplication relationships between tasks, memories, memoirs, workflow steps, and handoffs.

edge_id.description: Unique identifier for this dependency edge. Examples: 'edge-01HWQM3P7X6Y2N4R8T5V0K9J1D', 'dep-task-a-blocks-task-b'.

edge_type.description: Type of dependency relationship. 'blocks' = from blocks to; 'relates_to' = bidirectional relevance; 'discovered_from' = to was discovered via from; 'supersedes' = from replaces to; 'duplicates' = from and to are the same.

from_id.description: Unique identifier of the source entity.

to_id.description: Unique identifier of the target entity.

defer_until.description: ISO 8601 timestamp. If set, this edge is deferred until this moment. Null if not deferred.

due_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp. When this edge (e.g., blocking relationship) becomes critical. Null if no deadline.

metadata.description: Optional metadata. May include 'priority', 'reason', 'context', or other producer-specific fields.
